query: LAG, LEAD, ROW_NUMBER, RANK, SUM OVER, AVG OVER partitioned by region

pandas.note: pandas not installed: No module named 'pandas'

validation.concept: Vectorized window operators vs interpreted groupby (pandas) vs Volcano iterator (Postgres)
validation.proof: DuckDB 598,587 rows/sec vectorized window; DuckDB ops: LAG, LEAD, ROW_NUMBER, RANK, SUM/AVG OVER
validation.validates: Lecture 11 claim: window functions require sorted partition state — DuckDB fuses sort + aggregate operators in a single vectorized pass; pandas requires extra sort + merge join; Postgres uses tuple-at-a-time iterator
validation.interpretation: DuckDB executes 7 window operators (LAG, LEAD, ROW_NUMBER, RANK, SUM OVER, AVG OVER, delta) in a single sorted-partition scan at 598,587 rows/sec. Pandas requires a separate sort pass per groupby key plus a merge join to attach partition aggregates — it has no operator fusion. Postgres uses a Volcano iterator model that materializes each window frame individually. L11 key insight: 'window sort is the same sort — fuse partitioning into a single pass.'
